title: Commercial & Gate Pages | Hybrid SEO/Product Page Build
assignees: Dorcas Tiche, Isaac Faseyiku, Mubeen Dildar-Mia, Mvelo Nsibanyoni
description:
Seeking help w/ the request below please.
 
The client has provided a pre-built XML template and a structured content spreadsheet to create city-specific landing pages for two service lines:
• Commercial Garage Doors
• Gates

The dev team has already imported the XML into WordPress, creating 4 master template pages:
• rwgaragedoors.com/commercial-door_master-template-1/
• rwgaragedoors.com/commercial-door_master-template-2/
• rwgaragedoors.com/gate_master-template-1/
• rwgaragedoors.com/gate_master-template-2/

40 images have also been uploaded to the media library.

─────────────────────────────
⚠️ IMPORTANT CONTEXT (Per Corey)
─────────────────────────────
This is NOT a typical local SEO content build. The client's XML and spreadsheet should be treated more like an e-commerce product catalog. Right Way Garage Doors operates as a hybrid business — they provide services AND sell physical products (doors, springs, lifters, etc.).

This means the landing pages need to be approached like product pages, not just local service pages. The SEO strategy and content optimization should reflect both the service and product components.

The content in the spreadsheet was already written by the client, and they said it serves as a starting point. Use it as-is and populate it into the appropriate template fields (CSS class IDs map content to specific sections).

─────────────────────────────
📁 ASSETS PROVIDED
─────────────────────────────
• Excel spreadsheet: City-by-city content for all landing pages (headings, descriptions, etc. mapped to CSS class IDs)
• XML file: WordPress template (already imported)
• 2 templates for Commercial Doors, 2 for Gates — cities alternate between Template 1 and Template 2
• ~27 cities per service line (54 pages total)

─────────────────────────────
📝 NEXT STEPS
─────────────────────────────
1. Review the 4 live template pages and the content spreadsheet
2. Flag any SEO optimizations needed, given the hybrid service/product nature of the pages
3. Begin populating city-specific pages using the spreadsheet content
4. Note: Client verification is pending but work can begin internally based on assets provided

─────────────────────────────
💬 NOTES
─────────────────────────────
• Corey has flagged this as a unique case that differs from standard local SEO — please align your approach accordingly before executing. Please CC him on the task.




The Dev team already migrated the template to the site thru this task: Re: Proceed with the importing of the code template - J415 Right Way Garage



Thanks much!


cc

title: Create Warranty Page
description:
Seeking help w/ the client request below:
https://www.rwgaragedoors.com/

The client is requesting a new warranty page be created at rwgaragedoors.com/warranty. They have provided full page copy and a detailed warranty claim form with all required fields, validation rules, and submission behavior. The page should also be added as a new tab in the main navigation. Marina Garcia confirmed the URL and provided all content in her most recent reply. 

Client Goal: Create a fully functional warranty page with supporting copy and a warranty claim submission form, accessible via a new navigation tab.

 
Requirements for Completion:
 
New page URL:
rwgaragedoors.com/warranty

Page & Navigation:
  • New warranty page is live at rwgaragedoors.com/warranty
  • Warranty page is added as a new tab in the main navigation, consistent with existing navigation styling (client provided a reference image of the nav structure)
Page Copy: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HV5jdRJB7kfSihtRKr5lctyYuTjaeNgwthjyj0Spe7Y/edit?usp=sharing

Warranty Claim Form: The form must include the following fields:
  • Name* (required)
  • Address* (required)
  • Email* (required)
  • Phone* (required)
  • Preferred Contact Method* (required) — options: Call, Text, Email
  • Best Time to Contact (optional)
  • Job or Estimate Number (optional)
  • Is the garage door currently operational?* (required) — options: Yes, Partially, No
  • Description of Issue* (required)
  • Attachments (optional) — supported file types: JPEG, JPG, PNG; video uploads up to 50MB

Form Behavior:
  • All warranty submissions are automatically emailed to: WARRANTIES@RWGARAGEDOORS.COM
  • Email subject/title auto-populates with the customer's name and address if possible
  • Auto-response is sent to the customer upon submission with the following message:
    • "Thank you for submitting your warranty request. Our team has received your information and will review your claim. A representative will contact you as soon as possible regarding the next steps. If your garage door is unsafe to operate, please discontinue use immediately."

Safety Notice:
  • A safety notice section must appear on the page: "If your garage door is off track, hanging unevenly, making loud noises, or difficult to open or close, discontinue use immediately until the system can be professionally inspected."
 
Place Warranty in Navigation bar


QA:
  • Form is tested end-to-end and submissions route correctly to WARRANTIES@RWGARAGEDOORS.COM
  • Auto-response is confirmed sending upon submission
  • Navigation tab appears correctly across desktop and mobile
  • Page formatting is consistent with the rest of the site
 
Thanks much!

description:

  • Device/browser: desktop and mobile

title: New page template update
description:
Hi this is related to this task - Proceed with the importing of the code template - J415 Right Way Garage

The client provided feedback for changes on the imported pages.

*For the images for hero section we don't have one yet but I already asked their AM for it. But if there is an image that DEV can use please let us know too.
 
For the commercial door templates:
https://www.rwgaragedoors.com/commercial-door_master-template-1/
https://www.rwgaragedoors.com/commercial-door_master-template-2/

  1. Change the background hero image to one that reflects commercial doors. 
  2. There should be a background overlay on top of the hero image so the PNG roll up door and hero content could pop visually.
  3. Change the font color of the hero title and copy to white.
  4. The form fill should draw the visitor’s eyes. Make the title background RW gold/yellow. Reduce the size of the description/copy font in the form to 0.8em or a font size that’s roughly 80% of the current font size.
  5. The form fields should have a gray outline to differentiate and create visual boundaries and spacing.
  6. Add a submit button that is RW gold/yellow with black font.
  7. Increase Google map height by about 150% of its current height. Set a max height of approximate 20-25% vh
  8. The “Industries we Service” section should have a RW gold/yellow section background to create visual separation and spacing between sections.
  9. The “Why Business Choose Right Way” should have a transparent white background overlay on top of the background image. This will allow the copy pop as it’ll increase contrast. 
  10. The FAQ section should have a transparent black background set to about 85% and the tilting and copy font color changed to white.
 
For the gate template,
https://www.rwgaragedoors.com/gate_master-template-1/
https://www.rwgaragedoors.com/gate_master-template-2/

1. Same request for the hero section of the landing page.
  • Change the background hero image to one that reflects commercial doors]

2. Same request for the form fill.
  • The form fill should draw the visitor’s eyes. Make the title background RW gold/yellow. Reduce the size of the description/copy font in the form to 0.8em or a font size that’s roughly 80% of the current font size.
 
3. Same request for the map.
  • Increase Google map height by about 150% of its current height. Set a max height of approximate 20-25% vh

4. Same request for the “Why Residents & Businesses Choose Right Way” as above.
  • The “Why Business Choose Right Way” should have a transparent white background overlay on top of the background image. This will allow the copy pop as it’ll increase contrast. 

5. Same request for the FAQ as on the commercial door landing page.
  • The FAQ section should have a transparent black background set to about 85% and the tilting and copy font color changed to white.

 
---
Thanks much!

cc

title: Mobile Pop-Up CTA — Feasibility Check
description:
During the Right Way Garage Doors onboarding welcome call (April 20, 2026), the client verbally requested a mobile-specific pop-up call-to-action to be implemented on their website. The client's goal is to improve conversion rates by proactively engaging mobile visitors at critical moments — particularly capturing leads that might otherwise leave the site without taking action, including inquiries that come in outside of business hours. The request came from the client side and carries an expectation that it will be evaluated and acted on. Is this feasible or within scope? 


Key Questions / Clarifications Needed:

  • Is it technically feasible to implement a timed mobile pop-up that triggers after 20–30 seconds of visitor engagement on the site?
  • Can the pop-up dynamically switch from a call button to an online booking button after 8pm?
  • If feasible, is this considered in-scope or would it require a change order?
  • Are there any platform or plugin constraints on rwgaragedoors.com we should be aware of before committing to the client?

Relevant Client Request (verbal, captured from onboarding call): The client proposed a mobile pop-up CTA that activates after a visitor has been on any page for 20–30 seconds. During business hours it should display a call button; after 8pm it should automatically switch to an online scheduling/booking button to capture late inquiries outside of business hours. Right Way currently handles 1,400–1,600 calls per month and has a stated goal of increasing lead volume by 10% within three months — this feature is seen as a key lever for improving on-site conversion to support that target.

https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KP8VHTKF4E5B98QYJMCVZDED?t=1523.78

CC:

title: SEO & Technical Audit Deliverable — Scope & Timeline Confirmation
description:
During the Right Way Garage Doors onboarding welcome call (April 20, 2026), Sandi explicitly requested a detailed audit summary upon completion of the comprehensive SEO and technical audit currently underway. Fed confirmed on the call that the audit — including an LLM (AI search exposure) analysis — is already in progress. The client has a clear expectation that findings will be delivered with actionable recommendations. 

Before a PM ticket can be created, we need SEO to confirm scope, delivery format, and timeline.


Key Questions / Clarifications Needed:

  • Where does the audit currently stand and what is the expected completion date?
  • What will the deliverable look like — is there a standard audit report format, or does this need to be built out?
  • Will the audit cover all of the following as the client expects? 
    • Baseline keyword rankings
    • SEO gaps and opportunities
    • Competitor benchmarking
    • LLM/AI search exposure analysis
    • Content pruning recommendations
    • New content creation opportunities
  • Is there anything the client needs to provide to support the audit findings?
  • What is the recommended timeline for delivering findings back to the client?

Relevant Client Request (verbal, captured from onboarding call): The client — specifically Sandi — requested a detailed audit summary that establishes baseline keyword rankings, identifies SEO gaps, and benchmarks Right Way against competitors. The expectation is that findings come back with clear recommendations on what content should be pruned, what new content should be created, and where the biggest ranking opportunities exist. Right Way has a stated goal of a 10% increase in call volume within three months, and the audit findings are expected to directly inform the SEO strategy that supports that target.

Meeting Soundbite: https://app.fireflies.ai/soundbites/3vZLihvQW7N

CC:

title: GBP Updates and Changes Being Made
description:
Hey ,

We have seen more and more edits to the client's GBPs since we onboarded them, and would like to get ahead of this.

This was/is not or I, as we always finish our GBP audit first, then proceed with the GBP optimization task based on the audit findings. (unless there is an urgent item that needs attending to, but recording and tracking is always documented)

However, this makes it challenging not only to fully complete the audit with accurate, up-to-date info, as things are constantly changing, but also to optimize the GBPs, ensuring they stay aligned with our strategies.

As the client's experts, we always ask clients not to touch the GBPs and allow us to do so. But, if they have changes they want made, that they either:
  •  Send us the desired changes, and we implement
OR
  • Always run it by us first, then we can provide them with direction on how to proceed, any edits needed, etc.

There are several reasons why it is crucial for them to follow, but generally speaking, changes can pose the risk of reverification/suspension, and/or negatively impact results, SEO, etc.

So, can we please verify whether the client is aware of these changes being made and educate them on the above, so we can ensure proper alignment moving forward? :)

Let me know if you have any questions or need clarification, help, or etc. I am more than happy to jump in with ya!

GBP Change Log
Started with Hayman edits 5/10 - Re: GBP Changelog - DEV TEAM
Pleasant Hill edits 5/13 - Re: GBP Changelog - DEV TEAM
